findings.title: EU-locale CSV import (semicolon delimiter + decimal comma) silently shatters every rate into garbage
findings.rootCause: RatesEditor.parseCsv splits each line on the regex /[,;\t]/ (line 478). For a EU-locale cell like `0,88` the comma is the DECIMAL separator, but the split treats it as a field delimiter, so `USD;0,88;0,90` becomes ['USD','0','88','0','90']. parseRatesGrid then maps the year columns onto the shards: USD/2020 = 88, GBP/2021 = 1, GBP/2020 = 17 (empirically confirmed via probe). The locale-aware decimal-comma logic in manualRates.parseRateAmount is defeated because parseCsv has already torn the comma out before parseRatesGrid sees the cell. The .xlsx path (SheetJS typed cells) is immune, but the .csv path — which is exactly what 'Download template' produces and what most users round-trip — is broken for every comma-decimal locale (pt/es/fr/de…).

findings.title: Auto-fill and template pin a bogus 1:1 FX (1 unit = 1 EUR) for no-live-rate currencies (ARS/EGP/VND/TRY…)
findings.rootCause: computeAutoRate's FX fallback is `convertFn(1, cur, home)` (manualRates.ts:191) and the placeholder uses `convertCurrency(1, cur, home)` (RatesEditor.tsx:213). convertCurrency silently returns the input unchanged for unknown codes (_rateFor → `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1`, currency.ts:77), so the result is 1, which passes the `rate > 0` / `Number.isFinite && r>0` guards. Neither the editor, computeAutoRate, nor the placeholder consults hasRate(), unlike presentValue.ts which carefully routes no-rate currencies to Model B. Confirmed via probe: computeAutoRate('fx','ARS',currentYear,[],undefined,'EUR',convertFn)===1 (and also ===1 for 2015).

findings.title: Download template bakes auto values as if user-entered — incl. past-year FX = today's rate — so a round-trip silently freezes wrong overrides
findings.rootCause: currentValueForTemplate (RatesEditor.tsx:447) calls computeAutoRate for any blank cell with NO per-year guard. The auto-fill button deliberately skips past-year FX cells that have no expenses (RatesEditor.tsx:413-419, the PV4-2 guard: today's rate is a misleading baseline for an old year), but the template path reintroduces exactly that value. On re-import every populated cell is written to draft and Save persists it as a hard override (writeMerged), converting auto cells into permanent pins.

findings.title: Deleting a year column or currency row clears the dirty flag, stranding other unsaved edits (Save disabled, silent loss on navigate)
findings.rootCause: removeYear (line 354) and removeCurrency (line 374) unconditionally call setDirty(false) after persisting only the deleted row/column. They don't account for other cells the user has edited in the draft. The Save button is gated on `disabled={!dirty}` (line 827), so clearing dirty disables it while unsaved edits remain in `draft`.

findings.title: Switching the FX↔Inflation Personalization tab discards unsaved rate edits with no confirmation
findings.rootCause: Personalization renders the editor conditionally per tab (`{tab === 'fx' ? <RatesEditor mode="fx"/> : null}`), so switching tabs unmounts the component and its local `draft` state. Edits live only in component state until Save (by design, RatesEditor.tsx:24-25), and nothing blocks/preserves them across an unmount.

findings.title: Importing a well-formed FX file that only contains the home-currency row shows a false 'couldn't read that file' error
findings.rootCause: applyParsedCells skips every cell where `isFx && currency === home` (lines 488, 498) since FX has no home row, so `applied` stays 0. onImportFile treats `applied === 0` as a hard failure and calls setImportError(true) (lines 532-534), conflating 'nothing applicable' with 'unreadable file'.
